ground_truth.title: 39 Best Electrician Tools for Your Tool Belt & Your Business in 2025
ground_truth.author: ServiceTitan
ground_truth.main_content: Electricians need specialist tools to do their job effectively and safely, so their tool kit should include a range of heavy-duty, insulated hand tools and high-tech tools they can use wherever their work takes them. Beyond the practical tools needed to do their job, there are also software tools available to help electrical contractors manage their business and juggle the everyday demands of taking calls, scheduling bookings, dispatching techs to jobs, and marketing the business. Below, we review what we consider to be the 39 best electrician tools and apps in two categories: Tools electricians need to do their job, and Tools to help electricians grow and manage their business. The article covers hand tools including pliers, cutting tools, screwdrivers, crimpers, tape measures, electrical tape, cable ties, electric drills, levels, wire/cable lugs, coax connectors, wire strippers, fish tape, fishing rods, terminal blocks, electrical testers, voltage testers, multimeters, clamp meters, reaming bits, conduit benders, splicing connectors, and flashlights. Safety equipment covered includes insulated gloves and fire-resistant work shirts. Business management software tools are also reviewed for helping electricians manage and grow their electrical business.
with: Electricians need specialist tools to do their job effectively and safely, so their tool kit should include a range of heavy-duty, insulated hand tools and high-tech tools they can use wherever their work takes them.
Pliers — often referred to as cutting pliers or lineman's pliers — are a staple on any electrician's tool belt.
An electrical tester is a tool kit staple. This can be used as a locator of electrical conditions rather than a device to measure voltage.
Electricians need a handheld voltage tester to test outlets for power, so they know when they're safe to work on and also to confirm power has been restored.
Beyond the practical tools needed to do their job, there are also software tools available to help electrical contractors manage their business.
